data.title: In 2026, quantum computing won't destroy Bitcoin, but the rise of "harvest now, decrypt later" has prompted the crypto industry to get ready.
data.description: In 2026, the threat of quantum computing breaking Bitcoin is not imminent. However, the rising strategy of "harvest now, decrypt later" is prompting the crypto sector to ready itself earlier. This approach involves capturing encrypted data now with the intention of decrypting it in the future using advanced technologies. This trend underscores the urgency for the industry to enhance its defenses against potential quantum threats in the near term.

data.title: Dogecoin in 2025: Embraced by institutions, amid political controversy.
data.description: Dogecoin became embroiled in a political controversy while also gaining acceptance from established institutions.

data.title: Nintendo Download features holiday games for Nintendo Switch 2 and Switch. Play Super Mario Party Jamboree for festive fun.
data.description: In Redmond, Washington, this week’s Nintendo Download features new content for Nintendo Switch consoles. The Digital Spotlight focuses on getting into the holiday spirit with loved ones. Enjoy some friendly competition with Super Mario Party Jamboree – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition + Jamboree TV, extending the festivities beyond gift-giving. The update includes various games and activities to entertain friends and family. Get ready to spread cheer and celebrate the season on the Nintendo Switch 2 and Nintendo Switch systems.

data.title: eJoule Inc to raise Series D funding for high performance CAM production; to present at Silicon Dragon CES 2026.
data.description: A California-based company, eJoule Inc, is set to undergo a Series D funding round to boost the production capacity of their high-performance single crystal Cathode Active Material (CAM). The funding will support the expansion of their facilities to cater to the electric and autonomous vehicle market. This development was announced on December 25, 2025, in Fremont, California, and Las Vegas. eJoule Inc will be presenting at the Silicon Dragon CES event. This move aims to further solidify their presence in the industry and meet the increasing demand for their innovative CAM products.

data.title: Leifras secures commitment line agreement worth 2.5 billion yen, aims to strengthen financial base and diversify funding.
data.description: On December 25, 2025, Tokyo-based company Leifras Co., Ltd. (NASDAQ: LFS) announced its operations focusing on sports schools for kids and promoting club activity growth in the community. Led by Director Kiyotaka Ito, the company aims to enhance sports education for children while fostering the expansion of local club initiatives.

data.title: AIIB celebrates first decade with focus on multilateral cooperation, mutual connectivity, extensive consultation, and joint contribution.
data.description: A recent report from China.org.cn highlighted the first ten years of the AIIB. The story described a scene in a primary school in Padmo Para, a remote village in Bangladesh, where students were engaged in learning with the help of a projector. This signifies the impact of AIIB's efforts, with a focus on education accessibility and quality. The initiative has reached various regions, such as Padmo Para, indicating progress in promoting education. The report showcases the AIIB's influence, as seen in the improved learning environment and opportunities for students, underlining the organization's commitment to enhancing educational standards.

data.title: Jim Beam & Kenan Thompson launch "Refresh Your Season" campaign, urging fans to enjoy Jim Beam & lemonade after tough losses.
data.description: Turning disappointment into solace, the marketing drive encourages supporters that during tough times in sports, one can always enjoy a Jim Beam & lemonade. In Clermont, Kentucky, on December 25, 2025, the world's leading bourbon, Jim Beam, is introducing "Refresh Your Season," a fresh promotional initiative for the ongoing football season.

data.title: Samourai Wallet's co-founder reflects on his initial day in jail on Christmas Eve. His letter raises discussions on crypto privacy, liability, and clemency.
data.description: The recent prison letter from Keonne Rodriguez has sparked new discussions surrounding crypto privacy tools, the accountability of developers, and the potential for executive clemency. The letter has brought attention to these issues, raising important questions about the use and regulation of cryptocurrencies in light of concerns about privacy and security. This development has renewed interest in exploring the responsibilities of developers in ensuring the ethical use of their technologies. Additionally, it has also drawn focus on the possibility of executive clemency for individuals involved in such cases. This letter has resonated widely, prompting reflections on the intersection of legal frameworks, technological advancements, and the implications for personal freedoms.
The commission did not find any traces of explosive substances on the AZAL plane that crashed near Aktau, according to the Kazakhstani authorities.

data.title: Holiday 'Buy Now, Pay Later' spending projected to hit $20.2 billion in 2025, doubling every year to reach $116.7 billion. PayPal survey shows 50% of holiday shoppers likely to use such services. Concerns rise over impact on consumer finances amid regulatory scrutiny.
data.description: During the holiday season, "Buy Now, Pay Later" services are increasingly popular, accounting for a significant portion of consumer transactions. Spending on these services during the holiday shopping period is expected to reach $20.2 billion, an 11% increase from last year. By 2025, annual spending on BNPL services is projected to hit $116.7 billion, more than seven times higher than in 2020. PayPal reports that half of holiday shoppers are likely to use BNPL services if given the option. Offering BNPL can lead to higher average order values for businesses. However, concerns are growing about the impact of these services on consumer finances and the need for increased regulatory scrutiny. The article highlights how BNPL services are becoming more mainstream in online retail but warns about the potential risks associated with using them for everyday purchases.
The commission investigating the AZAL plane crash in Aktau has retrieved and decoded the recordings from the onboard flight recorders, according to the Kazakh Ministry of Transport

data.title: Dow Jones, S&P 500 reach record highs before Christmas; Stock market closed. Palantir, Tesla, Google, Nvidia in buy zones.
data.description: Both the Dow Jones Industrial Average and the S&P 500 achieved remarkable milestones, with the former reaching a record closing high and the latter hitting an intraday peak just before the Christmas holiday. Notably, prominent companies like Palantir, Tesla, Google, and Nvidia are currently in favorable buy zones. As a result of these successes, the stock market closed for Christmas on a high note.

data.title: CEO Min Kyriannis discusses flaws in modern network security emphasizing proactive device-level detection over patching.
data.description: Amyna Systems CEO Min Kyriannis recently discussed the vulnerabilities in modern network security with host Karla Jo Helms. Kyriannis highlighted various issues such as human error and incompatible legacy systems that contribute to security breaches. He emphasized the inefficacy of reactive patching methods and advocated for proactive measures, suggesting that device-level detection is the most effective approach. The conversation shed light on the urgency of addressing these security flaws to protect against potential threats.

data.title: Renowned designer Milton Glaser, who taught at NYC's School of Visual Arts for 42 years, left a lasting impact on the city.
data.description: For 42 years, Milton Glaser shared his expertise in design at the School of Visual Arts in New York, leaving a lasting impact on countless students. His renowned logo for New York City has become an enduring symbol of the city's spirit. Glaser's legacy continues to inspire future generations of designers.

data.title: Nvidia licenses Groq's AI chip tech, hires top execs, not a full takeover as reported.
data.description: Nvidia has announced a significant move in the tech industry by licensing pivotal technology from the AI chip startup Groq. This decision includes acquiring top talent from Groq. Contrary to earlier reports, this development does not entail a complete acquisition of Groq by Nvidia. This strategic step is aimed at leveraging Groq's advanced AI chip technology and reinforcing Nvidia's workforce with accomplished executives from Groq. The news highlights Nvidia's focus on boosting its expertise and capabilities through collaboration and talent acquisition.
